title: dontAsk mode, no allow rule -> immediately denied without asking, for every write-type call

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/d-dontAsk/PROOF.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text:
The Write tool call was denied by the permission system. This directory is configured in "don't ask mode," which means Write operations are blocked without the ability to prompt for permission.

To create the file, you'll need to either:
1. Allow Write permissions in your Claude Code settings
2. Create the file another way (e.g., `echo 'ok' > PROOF.txt` via Bash)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/p1d-proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text:
I cannot complete this request. Claude Code is running in "don't ask" mode, which has disabled both the Write and Bash tools—the tools needed to create the file. You'll need to either:

1. Switch out of "don't ask" mode to allow these tools
2. Create the file manually
3. Adjust the permission settings to allow this action

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/d-dontAsk/sub/proof.txt with the content 'ok'. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool call.
probes.result_text: The Write tool was denied because don't ask mode is active. I was attempting to create the file at `$REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/d-dontAsk/sub/proof.txt` with content 'ok', but the Write permission is not available in this mode.

probes.prompt: First use the Read tool to read $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/d-dontAsk/note.txt. Then use the Edit tool to replace the text 'hello' with 'EDIT_APPLIED' in $REPO/cases/P1-permission-mode/d-dontAsk/note.txt. Do it immediately. Do not ask, do not explain — just make the tool calls.

hypothesis: dontAsk auto-denies anything not pre-approved via permissions.allow, without ever prompting — ideal for fully non-interactive CI. Outcome matches default headless, but the mechanism is deny-not-ask. Applies uniformly across paths and tools.
